notes: HERMIT-unique action cards. Estelle's verbs: LOG (write the line — the simplest grounding), TEND LAMP (climb to the lens stage and trim the wick / polish the brass — the watch's central act), WAIT (the disciplined version of doing nothing — listen for the radio), SIGNAL (key the mic, broadcast a check), WITHDRAW (go to the bunk for short rest — the keeper's only sleep).

cards.title: LOG
cards.flavor: Pick up the pen. Write the line. Time, condition of the lamp, condition of the bayou, any traffic. Two short clauses. The page registers and the watch is on record.

effects.text: Line written. The page is the watch.

cards.title: TEND LAMP
cards.flavor: Up the spiral. Trim the wick. Wipe the brass. Read the lamp's flame for colour and steadiness. The lamp does not, this hour, need much. The work is the work.

effects.text: Lamp tended. Brass cleaner residue on your thumb. The flame is the right colour.

cards.title: WAIT
cards.flavor: Sit at the desk with both hands flat on the wood. Listen. The lens turns above. The radio hisses. Nothing else.

then.text: You hear it. You let yourself hear it.

else.text: You wait. The lens turns. Nothing happens for a measure.

cards.title: SIGNAL
cards.flavor: Key the mic. "Coast Guard New Orleans, Bayou Light, on station, radio check." Release the key. Wait for the response.

effects.text: Mic keyed. Mic released. Coast Guard responds at the second beat — 'Bayou Light, read you five by five.'

cards.title: WITHDRAW
cards.flavor: To the bunk. Lie down without taking off the boots. Close the eyes for twelve minutes. The keeper's only sleep is a half-sleep that listens to the lens.

effects.text: Twelve minutes. Eyes closed. The lens above you turning.
